summary: The diff implements most of the requested requirements. REQ-1 through REQ-4 and REQ-6 appear well-covered. REQ-5 has a significant gap: the backend uses an AccessControl mixin/module rather than a simple hardcoded admin principal list in main.mo, and the constraint 'Admin principal must be set via a real Internet Identity principal — do not use a placeholder' cannot be verified from the truncated diff. The getAdminPrincipal() function is not visible in the diff. Additionally, a selfPromoteAdmin function appears in useQueries.ts which was not requested and may conflict with the requirement to hardcode the owner's principal.

missing_requirements.requirement: Hardcode the app owner's Internet Identity principal as the initial admin; expose getAdminPrincipal() returning the admin principal
missing_requirements.reason: The backend/main.mo diff is truncated and delegates admin logic to an AccessControl mixin module (authorization/access-control). The getAdminPrincipal() function is not visible anywhere in the diff. The constraint requires the admin principal to be a real Internet Identity principal hardcoded in the single backend/main.mo actor file, but the implementation uses an external module. Cannot confirm a real principal is hardcoded vs. a placeholder.

hallucinated_features.description: selfPromoteAdmin mutation exposed in useQueries.ts and backend
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention a selfPromoteAdmin or self-promotion admin flow. The requirement explicitly states the admin principal must be hardcoded. Exposing a selfPromoteAdmin function contradicts the intent and was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: backend/migration.mo file added with a migration module
hallucinated_features.reason: No migration system or data migration requirement was mentioned in the build request. This is an additional feature not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: MixinAuthorization and MixinStorage modules included in backend/main.mo
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request requires all Motoko logic to remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file. Importing and using separate mixin modules (authorization/MixinAuthorization, blob-storage/Mixin, authorization/access-control, user-approval/approval) was not requested and may violate the constraint.
